debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
C : Category α
X Y : α
f : Hom X Y
⊢ @CategoryInContext.Category.split_mono α C Y X f ↔ ∀ (c : α), Function.Surjective fun g => comp g f

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
split mono, then it is a fibration.
    intro h
    have h₁ : ∀ c : α, Function.Surjective (fun g : Hom c X => g ≫ f) := by
      intro c
      -- Since f is a split mono, we can use the surjectivity of the composition.
      have h₂ : Function.Surjective (fun g : Hom c X => g ≫ f) := by
        -- Use the fact that f is a split mono to show surjectivity.
        have h₃ : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), IsSection f g := h
        rcases h₃ with ⟨g, hg⟩
        intro y
        use y ≫ g
        rw [← hg]
        <;> simp [comp_assoc]
        <;> rfl
      exact h₂
    exact h₁
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if f is a fibration, then it is a split mono.
    intro h
    -- Since f is a fibration, we can construct a section.
    have h₁ : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), IsSection f g := by
      -- Use the surjectivity of the composition to find a section.
      have h₂ : Function.Surjective (fun g : Hom Y X => g ≫ f) := by
        have h₃ := h Y
        exact h₃
      -- Use the surjectivity to find a section.
      have h₄ : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), g ≫ f = id Y := by
        have h₅ := h₂ (id Y)
        obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h₅
        refine' ⟨g, _⟩
        simpa [id_comp] using hg
      -- Construct the section.
      obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h₄
      refine' ⟨g, _⟩
      rw [IsSection]
      simpa [id_comp] using hg
    -- Conclude that f is a split mono.
    exact h₁
